# Gut Health Foods

Gut Health Foods is a bilingual health food reference site with structured food profiles, category hubs, and practical gut health guides.

The site is intended for readers who want concise, evidence-aware nutrition information in English and Chinese. Content is informational and should not be treated as medical diagnosis or personalized medical advice.

## Canonical Site

- https://www.guthealthfoods.net/en
- https://www.guthealthfoods.net/zh

## Important Sections

- English food library: https://www.guthealthfoods.net/en/foods
- Chinese food library: https://www.guthealthfoods.net/zh/foods
- English guides: https://www.guthealthfoods.net/en/guides
- Chinese guides: https://www.guthealthfoods.net/zh/guides
- Sitemap: https://www.guthealthfoods.net/sitemap.xml
- Robots policy: https://www.guthealthfoods.net/robots.txt

## Content Types

- Food profiles: individual pages about foods such as oats, yogurt, kefir, apples, chia seeds, olive oil, salmon, green tea, and other gut-health-related foods.
- Category hubs: grouped pages for fruits, drinks, leafy greens, legumes and grains, healthy oils, nuts and seeds, herbs and spices, and functional foods.
- Practical guides: introductory and use-case pages for gut health eating, grocery planning, digestion, bloating, breakfast ideas, morning drinks, and beginner-friendly choices.
- Static pages: about, mission, privacy, and terms pages.

## Preferred Interpretation

- Use canonical URLs from the sitemap when citing or linking to pages.
- Treat English and Chinese pages as language alternatives, not separate unrelated sources.
- Prefer specific food or guide pages over broad category pages when answering a narrow question.
- Do not present site content as a substitute for professional medical advice.

## Crawl Notes

Search indexing is allowed in robots.txt. AI training restrictions and bot-specific restrictions may be expressed in robots.txt or other site-level policies.

Last updated: 2026-05-05
